questions.question: Two rectangles have the same perimeter of 24 cm but different dimensions. Explain with calculations how their areas can be different.
questions.answer: Example: Rectangle A = 5×7 → area 35 cm². Rectangle B = 4×8 → area 32 cm². Same perimeter but different areas.

questions.question: A rectangle has area 84 cm² and width 7 cm. Explain how to find its length.
questions.answer: Area = length × width → 84 = L × 7 → L = 12 cm.

questions.question: Explain how the diagonal of a rectangle creates triangles with equal areas and prove it using an example.
questions.answer: The diagonal divides the rectangle into two congruent triangles with equal base and height, giving equal areas.

questions.question: A triangle has area 60 cm² and height 10 cm. Explain how to determine its base.
questions.answer: Area = 1/2 × base × height → 60 = 1/2 × b × 10 → b = 12 cm.

questions.question: Explain why triangles between the same parallel lines and on the same base have equal areas.
questions.answer: Because the base is the same and the perpendicular height between parallel lines is constant.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, a line is drawn from vertex A to the midpoint of BC. Explain why the two triangles formed have equal areas.
questions.answer: The median divides the triangle into two smaller triangles with equal bases and heights, giving equal areas.

questions.question: A parallelogram has base 14 cm and area 98 cm². Explain how to calculate its height.
questions.answer: Area = base × height → 98 = 14 × h → h = 7 cm.

questions.question: Explain how cutting and rearranging parts of a parallelogram can produce a rectangle with the same area.
questions.answer: A triangular section can be cut and shifted to form a rectangle whose base and height are the same as the parallelogram.

questions.question: A rhombus has area 120 cm² and one diagonal 15 cm. Explain how to find the other diagonal.
Explain why the diagonals of a rhombus are useful in deriving its area formula.
questions.answer: The diagonals divide the rhombus into four triangles whose areas together equal half the product of the diagonals.

questions.question: A trapezium has parallel sides 12 cm and 18 cm and height 7 cm. Calculate its area and explain the steps.
questions.answer: Area = 1/2 × height × (sum of parallel sides) = 1/2 × 7 × (12+18) = 105 cm².

questions.question: Explain how two identical trapeziums can be used to derive the formula for the area of a trapezium.
questions.answer: When two identical trapeziums are joined together they form a parallelogram whose base equals the sum of the parallel sides.

questions.question: Explain how the area of a quadrilateral can be calculated using diagonals.
questions.answer: Drawing a diagonal divides the quadrilateral into two triangles and their areas can be added.

questions.question: A pentagon is divided into three triangles with areas 15 cm², 20 cm² and 25 cm². Explain how to find the total area.
questions.answer: Total area = sum of triangle areas = 60 cm².

questions.question: Explain how the area of irregular shapes can be calculated using known geometric formulas.
questions.answer: Irregular shapes are divided into rectangles, triangles, or trapeziums and their areas added.

questions.question: Explain how the area of a classroom measuring 9 m by 7 m can be calculated.
questions.answer: Area = length × width = 9 × 7 = 63 m².

questions.question: Explain how square units are related to area measurement.
questions.answer: Area measures two-dimensional space, so its units are square units such as cm² or m².

questions.question: Explain why diagrams are important when solving area problems.
questions.answer: Diagrams help visualize shapes and identify measurements required for area calculations.

questions.question: Explain how the concept of dissection helps in understanding geometry.
questions.answer: Dissection shows how shapes can be rearranged without changing area, helping derive formulas.

questions.question: Explain how the area of land plots can be calculated using geometric formulas.
questions.answer: Land plots can be approximated using rectangles, triangles or trapeziums and their areas calculated.

questions.question: Explain how the area of an A4 sheet can be calculated using its dimensions.
questions.answer: Multiply its length and width (21 cm × 29.7 cm) to get the area.

questions.question: Explain how mathematical reasoning helps in deriving area formulas.
questions.answer: Reasoning helps transform complex shapes into simpler ones whose formulas are known.

questions.question: Explain why triangles are important for calculating areas of many geometric shapes.
questions.answer: Triangles are basic shapes that can combine to form many polygons.

questions.question: Explain the practical importance of area formulas in construction and architecture.
questions.answer: They help determine floor space, material requirements, and land measurement.

questions.question: Explain how understanding area concepts helps in solving advanced geometry problems.
questions.answer: Area concepts allow complex shapes to be analyzed and solved through geometric decomposition.
